access.accessMethod: Read the pinned XML in the CME git submodule.
access.entityLabel: General Corpus source XML for afy7823

entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afy7823

rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE contains the Unlicense and repeatedly describes the dedicated subject as software. It does not expressly establish that the repository has authority to dedicate inherited U-M XML, transcription, or markup, so the repository assertion is recorded without treating it as conclusive for this data artifact.
rights.component: Pinned CME submodule XML input, including inherited U-M transcription and markup
notes: The current U-M item-level No Copyright - United States assertion is recorded separately on the encoding entity.

access.accessMethod: Open the exact Quod item through the public CME browse page and select any section; the linked section route delivers the current full text and item rights. Direct automated requests to the stable resolver or all-in-one full-text route may receive a Cloudflare challenge.
access.entityLabel: University of Michigan CME item AFY7823.0001.001

entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR text encoded at Level 1 under the TEI in Libraries guidelines. It retains the edition title page, replaces forty-two preliminary pages with empty PB milestones, and serializes interleaved physical edition pages into reading-order XML.
entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AFY7823.0001.001

rights.basis: The current U-M item identifies its materials as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States.
rights.component: U-M digital text and public delivery
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not itself a license or worldwide public-domain determination.
The legacy XML header says that, where applicable, copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply; that historical caution is preserved rather than erased.

access.accessMethod: The XML preserves 284 historical TIFF filenames, but no current public image service or complete retrievable bundle was found at the stable-item or collection routes.
access.entityLabel: Historical U-M page-image set for AFY7823.0001.001

rights.basis: The current U-M item applies No Copyright - United States and the underlying 1875 edition is public domain in the United States. The historical TIFF set is not currently retrievable, and no separate image-specific license or delivery terms were verified.
rights.component: Historical U-M TIFF page-image production set for Horstmann's 1875 edition
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and does not establish a worldwide public-domain status or an open license.

access.accessMethod: Ask U-M Library about the historical 284-TIFF production set, OCR production records, and current reuse terms.
access.contact: University of Michigan Library Ask a Librarian
access.entityLabel: Historical U-M page-image set for AFY7823.0001.001

entities.description: The U-M production layer linked from the 2006 OCR. The XML header describes 284 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images, and its PB elements preserve a complete unique filename run 00000001.tif-00000284.tif. REF and SEQ diverge when interleaved physical edition pages are serialized into electronic reading order. No current public image endpoint or complete retrievable bundle was verified.
entities.label: Historical U-M page-image set for AFY7823.0001.001
notes: All 284 REF filenames and all 284 SEQ values are unique and consecutive; only 234 numeric REF values equal their corresponding SEQ because Ashmole/Egerton and Egerton/Laud physical page streams are serialized by reading order.
Unknown survival status records that current retrievability of the TIFF set has not been established; it does not imply that U-M destroyed the source files.

rights.basis: The current U-M item applies No Copyright - United States and the underlying 1875 edition is public domain in the United States. The historical TIFF set is not currently retrievable, and no separate image-specific license or delivery terms were verified.
rights.component: Historical U-M TIFF page-image production set for Horstmann's 1875 edition
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and does not establish a worldwide public-domain status or an open license.

access.accessMethod: Read or download the complete edition through the separately modeled Internet Archive/Google facsimile carrier; its exact cached derivatives are recorded under access:ia-horstmann-scan.
access.entityLabel: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden (1875)
access.format: Complete page-image PDF via facsimile carrier
notes: The edition-level access route does not identify the scholarly edition with the scan file; relation:ia-scan-of-edition records the carrier relationship.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (Paderborn: Ferdinand Schöningh, 1875).
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer

entities.description: The immediate printed source of the U-M encoding and the cataloging boundary: a German introduction of pp. i-xliv followed by six editorial divisions on pp. 1-240, printing ten complete or deliberately incomplete witness streams and selected variant apparatus.
entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden (1875)

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1875, so the applicable United States publication term has expired.
rights.component: 1875 editorial text, transcriptions as published, German introduction, notes, and typographical arrangement

rights.basis: The authoritative identity record establishes Horstmann's birth in 1851 but supplies no death date. Without a verified death date, this review cannot conclusively apply the United Kingdom life-plus-seventy term to his original editorial contribution.
rights.component: Horstmann's introduction, notes, selection, and other original editorial literary contribution
notes: The 1912 death date found for another Carl Horstmann belongs to an ophthalmologist and is not used here.

rights.basis: The United Kingdom twenty-five-year term for the typographical arrangement of this 1875 edition has expired, independently of the unresolved death date for Horstmann's literary contribution.
rights.component: Typographical arrangement of the 1875 published edition

access.accessMethod: Open the Internet Archive item or use the cached complete PDF, OCR, scandata, and metadata derivatives.
access.entityLabel: Internet Archive delivery of the Google/Harvard scan of Horstmann 1875

workPortion.label: Cataloged title page and six editorial divisions
notes: The German introduction on PDF pages 13-54 is source evidence but outside the cataloged content boundary except for the p. xliv transition represented by the XML.

workPortion.label: OCR of cataloged title page and six editorial divisions

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1875; the Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the US region.
rights.component: Underlying 1875 page content represented in the scan

rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive metadata supplies no open file license. The embedded Google terms describe the book as public domain for US users but request personal noncommercial use, attribution, retention of the watermark, and avoidance of automated querying; those delivery terms do not change the underlying edition's copyright status.
rights.component: Google scan, OCR, watermarks, and Internet Archive derivative-file presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms / worldwide status not established
notes: This record does not treat Google's requested use conditions as copyright in the underlying medieval or 1875 text.

entities.description: A complete 304-page scan of Harvard University's copy, digitized by Google and delivered by Internet Archive with OCR, scandata, and item metadata. The cataloged content appears on PDF page 11 and PDF pages 55-294.
entities.label: Internet Archive delivery of the Google/Harvard scan of Horstmann 1875

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1875; the Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the US region.
rights.component: Underlying 1875 page content represented in the scan

rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive metadata supplies no open file license. The embedded Google terms describe the book as public domain for US users but request personal noncommercial use, attribution, retention of the watermark, and avoidance of automated querying; those delivery terms do not change the underlying edition's copyright status.
rights.component: Google scan, OCR, watermarks, and Internet Archive derivative-file presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms / worldwide status not established
notes: This record does not treat Google's requested use conditions as copyright in the underlying medieval or 1875 text.

access.accessMethod: Consult under Bodleian Special Collections reading-room conditions; use the complete public facsimile for remote research.
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 108

dateStatements.label: Target hand and early core
dateStatements.value: late thirteenth or early fourteenth century

entities.description: Direct source of the Infancy poem at ff. 11ra-22rb and the Patrick/Purgatory version at ff. 96v-104r. The latter omits the opening life and begins with Purgatory.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 108

notes: The codex has later additions; both target passages belong to the early Hand A core.
The catalog localizes Hand A's scribal dialect to West Oxfordshire and describes an uncertain possible Oxford origin; neither is a proven composition place.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Infancy and Patrick/Purgatory wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography

access.accessMethod: Consult under Bodleian Special Collections reading-room conditions; use the complete public facsimile for remote research.
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 43

dateStatements.label: Manuscript date
dateStatements.value: first quarter of the fourteenth century

entities.description: Horstmann's 632-line Patrick base witness at ff. 34r-41v and his 278-line Conception of Mary version at ff. 208v-212r.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 43

notes: The catalog localizes the scribe's orthography to Gloucestershire, eLALME LP 7170; this is witness evidence, not a composition-place assertion.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Conception and Patrick wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography

access.accessMethod: Request the physical manuscript using the British Library collection-item request process; no current public complete facsimile was verified.
access.entityLabel: British Library Egerton MS 1993

entities.description: Direct source of the continuous Zur Geburt Jesu stream at ff. 27-40 and the complete Patrick stream at ff. 119v-128r. The first 276 encoded birth lines are DIMEV 4178; the following 897 are DIMEV 938.
entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1993

notes: The public catalog uses a historical description and supplies no current complete digital object.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and the three exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Conception, Expanded Nativity, and Patrick wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography

access.accessMethod: Consult under Bodleian Special Collections reading-room conditions; use the complete public facsimile for remote research.
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 779

dateStatements.label: Manuscript date
dateStatements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.description: Direct source of the 1,250-line Barlaam and Josaphat at visually verified ff. 288v-302r and a selective Patrick collation witness at ff. 10r-17r. The catalog and DIMEV Barlaam start f. 228v is a documented locator error.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 779

notes: The catalog associates the compiler's modified orthography with Hampshire, eLALME LP 5470; this is not the Barlaam poem's composition place.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms and the complete target Barlaam stream was validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Barlaam and Josaphat wording at ff. 288v-302r, excluding modern catalog description and photography

rights.basis: The underlying medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, whether or not Horstmann printed each reading in 1875.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann

rights.basis: Horstmann printed selected variants rather than the complete Bodley Patrick stream, and this review did not establish whether all remaining exact wording was lawfully made public before the end of 1988; the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work rule may therefore remain relevant until 31 December 2039.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
notes: The specific variants printed in 1875 are public domain; this conservative record concerns the unprinted remainder of the witness stream.

access.accessMethod: Consult under Bodleian Special Collections reading-room conditions; use the complete public facsimile for remote research.
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)

entities.description: Direct source of the Barlaam and Josaphat at ff. 100vb-103rc, with f. 102 lost, and Jesus and the Masters at current f. 301rc-vb. Horstmann's Jesus heading says f. 300, superseded by current catalog and image evidence.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)

physicalDescription.extent: viii + 342 + i surviving leaves; originally viii + 413
notes: Seventy-one leaves are lost from the original codex, including target f. 102 between the surviving Barlaam passages.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact surviving target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Jesus/Masters and surviving Barlaam wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography

access.accessMethod: Request the physical manuscript using the British Library collection-item request process; no current public complete facsimile was verified.
access.entityLabel: British Library Harley MS 4196

dateStatements.label: Manuscript date
dateStatements.value: fourth quarter of the fourteenth to first quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.description: Direct source of the expanded Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam and Josaphat at ff. 199vb-205vb, incomplete at the bottom of the final column.
entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4196

physicalDescription.extent: 258 leaves plus flyleaves, pastedown, and one parchment stub
notes: Current catalog origin: England.

rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and the exact surviving incomplete target stream was validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.
rights.component: Underlying surviving medieval Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography

access.accessMethod: Request the physical manuscript using the British Library collection-item request process; the legacy digital object is currently marked unavailable.
access.entityLabel: British Library Cotton MS Julius D IX

dateStatements.label: Manuscript date
dateStatements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

entities.description: A South English Legendary witness at ff. 36r-44v used selectively for Patrick variants under Horstmann's Ashmole base text; it is not a fourth complete printed stream.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Julius D IX

notes: Outer edges were damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.
Current catalog origin: England.

rights.basis: The underlying medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, whether or not Horstmann printed each reading in 1875.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann

rights.basis: Horstmann printed variants rather than a complete Cotton stream, and this review did not establish complete lawful publication before the end of 1988; the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work rule may remain relevant until 31 December 2039.
rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
notes: The specific variants printed in 1875 are public domain; this conservative record concerns the unprinted remainder of the witness stream.

access.accessMethod: Request the physical manuscript using the British Library collection-item request process; the legacy digital object is currently marked unavailable and the target Patrick leaves are lost.
access.entityLabel: British Library Harley MS 2277

entities.description: A circa-1300 South English Legendary witness discussed by Horstmann as one of the oldest surviving collections. Its opening leaves, including Patrick, are lost, so it supplies no extant target stream or target readings to the edition.
entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2277

notes: Current catalog origin: South-West England.
The manuscript is imperfect at the beginning; Horstmann reports the first twenty-four legends absent.

rights.basis: The target leaves and wording do not survive in the extant manuscript and supplied no target transcription or readings to Horstmann's edition. Rights in other surviving Harley 2277 contents are outside this manifest's target scope.
rights.component: Target Patrick wording formerly carried by lost opening leaves

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for the complete TIFF production set and any file manifest.
Request documentation of OCR, image-to-PB linking, and the REF-to-SEQ reading-order transformation.
Record any supplied files and contract terms as a new access layer without replacing the extant repository XML.
openQuestions.question: Can U-M supply the historical 284 TIFF production files, OCR workflow records, and current reuse terms for AFY7823.0001.001?

next_steps: Ask British Library Imaging Services to check for existing complete color files before commissioning new photography.
Identify ff. 27-40 and 119v-128r in Egerton, ff. 199vb-205vb in Harley 4196, and ff. 36r-44v in Cotton as target ranges while requesting complete-codex coverage separately.
Obtain written reuse and repository-redistribution terms before retaining or publishing supplied files.
openQuestions.question: Can complete current color facsimiles be obtained for Egerton MS 1993, Harley MS 4196, and Cotton MS Julius D IX?

next_steps: Send the providers canvas 470, canvas 596, and canvas 623 with the exact incipit, explicit, and Horstmann folio milestones.
Preserve both the claimed and corrected locators with provenance until provider records change.
openQuestions.question: Will the Bodleian catalog and DIMEV correct the Bodley 779 Barlaam start from f. 228v to the visually demonstrated f. 288v?

next_steps: Transcribe Horstmann's Patrick apparatus into a source-indexed collation table.
Use the complete Bodley facsimile and newly obtained Cotton images to verify each reading.
Continue modeling Cotton and Bodley as selective collation witnesses, not complete printed streams.
openQuestions.question: Which exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick readings did Horstmann collate, and can every apparatus note be assigned to a current folio and line?

next_steps: Monitor DIMEV and new manuscript identifications for another witness.
Keep Horstmann's dotted lacuna and resumed lineation explicit rather than silently joining f. 101v to f. 103r.
openQuestions.question: Can the approximately 480 verses lost with Vernon f. 102 be reconstructed from an independent copy of DIMEV 54?

next_steps: Ask the CME maintainer to state the intended license scope and provenance of authority for inherited corpus files.
Retain U-M item-level rights and the underlying 1875 text assessment separately from repository licensing.
Do not treat the software-oriented license wording as conclusive permission for all data layers without clarification.
openQuestions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense assertion validly cover the inherited U-M OCR, transcription, and XML markup in afy7823.xml?

next_steps: Query GND, VIAF, NACO, IdRef, and archival or university personnel records under exact authority identifiers.
Reject the ophthalmologist's 1847-1912 dates and GND 117531812 as an identity collision.
Reassess the UK literary and editorial copyright term only after a death date is supported.
openQuestions.question: Can an authoritative source establish the death date of the Anglist and editor Carl Horstmann, born 1851, without conflating him with the ophthalmologist Carl Wilhelm Horstmann?

next_steps: Identify later complete diplomatic editions or other lawful publications of both witness streams.
Separate variants printed by Horstmann in 1875 from the unprinted remainder.
Seek specialist UK copyright advice before publishing a new complete transcription of unprinted witness wording.
openQuestions.question: Was all exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick wording lawfully made public before the end of 1988, or can the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work term still affect unprinted witness text?

reviewNotes: The cataloging subject is Horstmann's exact edition-level compilation, not one medieval work and not the complete forty-four-page introduction.
The Bodley Barlaam locator ff. 288v-302r is a direct-image correction to the current catalog and DIMEV claim ff. 228v-302r; both claims retain explicit provenance.
DIMEV 5875 overlaps the f. 39v portion of DIMEV 938 and is not a ninth non-overlapping work.
Rights records distinguish medieval wording, 1875 editorial matter, U-M encoding, modern catalog data, Digital Bodleian photography, and scan-provider terms.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan digital item AFY7823.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding derived from Carl Horstmann's 1875 Altenglische Legenden. The encoded unit retains the title page, represents forty-two omitted preliminary pages as empty PB milestones, and encodes the six printed divisions on pp. 1-240. Those divisions contain eight non-overlapping intellectual texts through ten full or deliberately incomplete manuscript streams: Laud Infancy; Ashmole and Egerton Conception versions followed by the distinct Egerton Expanded Nativity; Bodley Barlaam; three Patrick/Purgatory versions; Vernon Jesus and the Masters; Vernon Barlaam; and Harley 4196 Barlaam. Horstmann also collated Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 selectively for Patrick and discussed Harley 2277 although its opening Patrick leaves were already lost. Four complete Digital Bodleian objects permit direct boundary verification; no complete current public images were found for the British Library witnesses. A complete Internet Archive/Google scan verifies the edition, while U-M's historically described 284 TIFF production images are not currently retrievable through the tested routes.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous Middle English poets, translators, and revisers
catalogSummary.date: Medieval texts attested in manuscripts of the late thirteenth through fifteenth centuries; compilation published 1875
catalogSummary.region: England (manuscript and linguistic context; no single composition place established)
catalogSummary.title: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer

catalogingSubject.label: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (1875 compilation)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The exact edition-level compilation represented by University of Michigan item AFY7823.0001.001: the title page and six editorial divisions on printed pages 1-240 of Carl Horstmann's 1875 Altenglische Legenden. The six divisions contain eight non-overlapping medieval intellectual texts represented through ten complete or deliberately incomplete printed witness streams, plus selected Patrick variants from two further witnesses and a comparison with a witness whose opening leaves are lost. This record does not collapse the compilation into one medieval work, count overlapping DIMEV record 5875 as a ninth text, transfer manuscript dates to the compilation, or identify the repository XML, U-M encoding, printed edition, manuscripts, and facsimiles as one object. Horstmann's forty-four-page German introduction is outside the encoded content unit except for its final errata page and is modeled as source evidence rather than as a ninth content item.

contentParts.label: I. Kindheit Jesu — The Apocryphal History of the Infancy
contentParts.summary: A Middle English infancy narrative follows Jesus, Mary, and Joseph through miracles in Egypt and episodes of the child's supernatural power; Horstmann prints the sole known Laud Misc. 108 witness.

contentParts.label: II. Zur Geburt Jesu

locators.value: pp. 62-110; Ashmole and Egerton physical pages are serialized in XML reading order

contentParts.summary: Horstmann interleaves Ashmole and Egerton versions of the Conception of Mary, then continues only the Egerton stream into the distinct Expanded Nativity.

contentParts.label: Conception of Mary

locators.value: pp. 64-81, parallel Ashmole and Egerton streams

notes: DIMEV describes a 280-line work; the encoded Horstmann streams contain 278 and 276 lines. The discrepancy is preserved rather than normalized.
contentParts.summary: A South English Legendary revision of the Marian section of DIMEV 6380, with an added prologue, represented by Ashmole and Egerton versions.

contentParts.label: A. Ashmole 43 version

locators.value: ff. 208v-212r; prologue ff. 208v-209v, main narrative ff. 209v-212r

contentParts.summary: The 278-line Ashmole version, including its prologue and Marian narrative.

contentParts.label: B. Egerton 1993 version, lines 1-276
notes: DIMEV's active record gives ff. 27-30 for this component; that endpoint does not delimit the following Expanded Nativity, which continues in the same manuscript through f. 40.
contentParts.summary: The opening 276 lines of Horstmann's continuous Egerton stream represent the Conception of Mary before the Expanded Nativity begins.

contentParts.label: Expanded Nativity
notes: DIMEV 5875 indexes an overlapping f. 39v passage inside this 897-line text; it is not counted separately.
contentParts.summary: A 897-line Egerton continuation narrates the Nativity and Christmas Gospel cycle, ending incomplete in the manuscript.

contentParts.label: III. Barlaam und Josaphat — Bodley version
notes: The current Bodleian catalog TEI and DIMEV v1.0 both say ff. 228v-302r. Direct inspection shows unrelated text on f. 228v/canvas 470, the exact Horstmann incipit beginning on f. 288v/canvas 596, and the explicit on f. 302r/canvas 623. The operative locator therefore preserves the visually verified correction while documenting the source error.
contentParts.summary: A South English Legendary Barlaam and Josaphat poem recounts Josaphat's conversion, instruction by Barlaam, trials, rule, renunciation, and sanctity; this version is distinct from both appendix versions.

contentParts.label: IV. Das Fegfeuer des heiligen Patrick

locators.value: pp. 149-211; full streams begin on pp. 151, 175, and 177

contentParts.summary: The South English Legendary life of Patrick and account of his Purgatory follows his mission in Ireland, the divinely revealed penitential pit, a knight's journey through punishments and paradise, and exhortations to penance. Horstmann prints three full versions and selected collateral variants.

contentParts.label: a. Ashmole 43 version
contentParts.summary: Horstmann's 632-line base stream, with Cotton Julius D IX variants and selected Bodley 779 readings in the apparatus.

contentParts.label: b. Egerton 1993 version
contentParts.summary: A complete 711-line Egerton version printed after the Ashmole base stream.

contentParts.label: c. Laud Misc. 108 Purgatory version
contentParts.summary: A 673-line Laud version that omits the opening life of Patrick and begins directly with the Purgatory; its first two lines are an addition according to Horstmann's manuscript note.

contentParts.label: Beilage I. Jesus and the Masters of the Law
notes: Horstmann's heading says f. 300; the current catalog, DIMEV, and direct images establish f. 301rc-vb.
contentParts.summary: At age twelve Jesus debates learned Jewish masters in the Temple, answers their challenges through Christian typology, and is found by Mary. The poem contains hostile medieval anti-Jewish polemic and is described here historically rather than affirmatively.

contentParts.label: Beilage II. Two further Barlaam and Josaphat poems
contentParts.summary: Horstmann places two distinct Middle English Barlaam and Josaphat poems side by side: a Vernon poem with a missing leaf and a Northern Homily Cycle poem from Harley 4196 that ends incomplete.

contentParts.label: A. Vernon Barlaam and Josaphat
notes: One dotted lacuna row at the break is not counted as a verbal verse line; Horstmann resumes numbering at 570 and ends at 792.
contentParts.summary: A distinct couplet version of Barlaam and Josaphat survives around the loss of Vernon f. 102; Horstmann estimates the missing leaf at roughly 480 verses.

contentParts.label: B. Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam and Josaphat
notes: One final dotted/incomplete display row is excluded from the verbal-line count.
contentParts.summary: A Northern Middle English couplet version from the expanded Northern Homily Cycle narrates Josaphat's conversion but breaks off at the bottom of the final surviving column.

notes: Publication date of the cataloged edition-level compilation, not a medieval composition date.

assertion.notes: Witness date only; it does not date either poem's composition.

dateStatements.display: Late thirteenth or early fourteenth century (Laud Misc. 108 target hand)
First quarter of the fourteenth century (Ashmole 43)

assertion.notes: The current public catalog does not support a narrower date; this is witness evidence for all three represented Egerton streams, not a shared composition date.

dateStatements.display: Fourth quarter of the fourteenth to first quarter of the fifteenth century (Harley MS 4196)
Second quarter of the fifteenth century (Bodley 779)

extent.description: Eight non-overlapping intellectual texts represented through fifteen structural records: six top-level editorial divisions, nested work boundaries, and version records. The XML contains 8,746 L elements in 111 LG elements; 8,744 are verbal verse lines, while one Vernon and one Harley dotted lacuna display each occupy an L row without verbal text. The 8,744 count is an edition-representation extent that includes parallel versions, not a claim that the eight abstract works total 8,744 unique canonical lines.

formStatements.description: All eight intellectual texts are verse; German prose headings, arguments, and notes are editorial paratext rather than prose content items.
Predominantly rhyming couplets or long-line couplets; DIMEV also records a terminal quatrain form for the Infancy poem.
Eight-line abababab stanzas, with a short irregular close in the printed representation.

scope.description: Title, headings, arguments, notes, and other editorial framing
Scattered Latin rubrics, biblical quotations, and brief formulae embedded across the otherwise Middle English catalog unit

notes: The XML language header records only ger, but the inspected content is primarily Middle English verse with German editorial framing and brief Latin and Anglo-Norman embeddings.

The 284 PB elements have unique consecutive REF filenames 00000001.tif-00000284.tif and unique consecutive SEQ values 1-284. REF and SEQ diverge where Horstmann's Ashmole/Egerton and Egerton/Laud physical pages are serialized into electronic reading order; this is a carrier-order transformation, not duplicate-image evidence.
Target-scoped validation is required because this record deliberately models one edition-level compilation rather than a synthetic witness census for every included medieval text.

next_steps: Consult work-specific scholarship beyond DIMEV and Horstmann for each text.
Keep witness dates and dialect localizations separate from composition claims.
Do not promote Horstmann's historical reconstruction of South English Legendary development into exact work dates without modern support.
openQuestions.question: What are the composition dates, places, and authorial or redaction histories of each of the eight medieval texts?

next_steps: Request complete color photography of Egerton MS 1993, identifying ff. 27-40 and 119v-128 separately.
Compare every boundary against Horstmann, DIMEV, and the physical foliation.
Retain ff. 27-40 for the whole Zur Geburt Jesu stream and the line-276/277 intellectual boundary until direct images support a refinement.
openQuestions.question: Can complete current images of Egerton MS 1993 verify the exact Conception/Expanded Nativity boundary, Patrick locus, and end conditions directly?

next_steps: Send Bodleian and DIMEV the canvas-470, canvas-596, and canvas-623 evidence with Horstmann's folio milestones.
Preserve both the claimed and corrected locators with provenance until the providers update their records.
openQuestions.question: Will the Bodleian catalog and DIMEV correct the Barlaam and Josaphat start from f. 228v to the visually demonstrated f. 288v?

next_steps: Transcribe Horstmann's Patrick apparatus into a source-indexed collation table.
Obtain Cotton Julius D IX images and use the complete Bodley 779 facsimile to verify each selected reading.
Continue modeling Cotton and Bodley as selective collation witnesses, not full printed streams.
openQuestions.question: Which exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick readings did Horstmann collate, and can every apparatus note be assigned to a current folio and line?

next_steps: Monitor DIMEV and new manuscript identifications for another witness.
Keep Horstmann's dotted lacuna and resumed lineation explicit rather than silently joining f. 101v to f. 103r.
openQuestions.question: Can the roughly 480 verses lost with Vernon f. 102 be reconstructed from an independent copy of DIMEV 54?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for the original TIFF production set, OCR workflow records, and current delivery or reuse terms.
Preserve the PB REF versus SEQ transformation separately from the current unavailability of the images.
openQuestions.question: Can U-M supply the historical 284 linked TIFF files and production records for the AFY7823 OCR item?

assertion.notes: This supports a broad discovery region only and does not transfer any witness production place or dialect localization into a composition claim.

scope.description: Manuscript and linguistic context of the represented medieval texts

assertion.notes: Horstmann assembled the six editorial divisions and their parallel witness streams as this 1875 volume.
The Patrick apparatus uses Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 selectively in addition to the three fully printed streams.
DIMEV describes the 1,854-line poem as translated from French; neither translator nor poet is identified.

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous South English Legendary poets and revisers
Anonymous poet of Jesus and the Masters of the Law
Anonymous Vernon Barlaam and Josaphat poet
Anonymous expanded Northern Homily Cycle poet

subjects.label: Medieval Jewish-Christian polemic
notes: The poem deploys hostile medieval anti-Jewish stereotypes; this neutral subject description does not endorse them.

summaries.value: Horstmann's 1875 compilation prints Middle English verse on the childhood and nativity of Jesus, three distinct Barlaam and Josaphat poems, the South English Legendary life of Patrick with his Purgatory in three full versions, and a Vernon debate between Jesus and learned masters. It juxtaposes parallel manuscript streams and selected variants rather than presenting one medieval authorial collection.

Six editorial divisions contain eight intellectual texts: one Infancy poem; the Conception of Mary in Ashmole and Egerton versions followed by the distinct Expanded Nativity in Egerton; a Bodley Barlaam and Josaphat; the Patrick/Purgatory poem in Ashmole, Egerton, and Laud versions; Jesus and the Masters from Vernon; and two further, mutually distinct Barlaam and Josaphat poems from Vernon and Harley 4196.

DIMEV 5875 describes a passage beginning on Egerton f. 39v inside the 897-line Expanded Nativity and is therefore an overlapping repertory description, not a ninth non-overlapping item. DIMEV 4178 and 938, by contrast, meet at a real boundary after Egerton line 276 and are modeled as two intellectual texts despite Horstmann's continuous Zur Geburt Jesu division.

notes: The punctuation is regularized from the title page; the four named groups encompass six editorial divisions and eight intellectual texts.
